# UC-10.11.28 · Palo Alto Panorama Push Failures

## Description

Push failures risk inconsistent security posture across managed firewalls.

## Value

Push failures risk inconsistent security posture across managed firewalls.

## Implementation

Forward Panorama commit and push job logs. Alert on failed pushes that leave devices out of sync with policy.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: `Splunk_TA_paloalto`.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `sourcetype=pan:panorama`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Forward Panorama commit and push job logs. Alert on failed pushes that leave devices out of sync with policy.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=pan sourcetype="pan:panorama" (status=failed OR result=error)
| stats count by device, job_id, admin
| sort -_time
```

Understanding this SPL

**Palo Alto Panorama Push Failures** — Push failures risk inconsistent security posture across managed firewalls.

Documented **Data sources**: `sourcetype=pan:panorama`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `Splunk_TA_paloalto`. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: pan; **sourcetype**: pan:panorama. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Detection type** for this use case: Anomaly — interpret thresholds and fields in that context.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=pan, sourcetype="pan:panorama". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by device, job_id, admin** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Optional CIM / accelerated variant (same use case, normalized fields via Common Information Model):

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Change.All_Changes by All_Changes.action, All_Changes.object_category, All_Changes.user | sort - count
```

Understanding this CIM / accelerated SPL

**Palo Alto Panorama Push Failures** — Push failures risk inconsistent security posture across managed firewalls.

Documented **Data sources**: `sourcetype=pan:panorama`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `Splunk_TA_paloalto`. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

This **CIM or accelerated** block uses normalized field names and/or `tstats` over data models. Enable **acceleration** on the referenced models (and correct CIM knowledge objects) or the search may return nothing.

**Detection type** for this use case: Anomaly — interpret thresholds and fields in that context.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Uses `tstats` against accelerated summaries for data model `Change.All_Changes` — enable acceleration for that model.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.


Step 3 — Validate
Run the search over a window that includes both normal production traffic and, if you have one, a documented test (vendor sandbox, lab rule hit, or exercise). Compare a few sample events to the vendor’s own console or reports and tune indexes, fields, and thresholds so alerts are actionable before paging.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (failed jobs), Timeline.

## SPL

```spl
index=pan sourcetype="pan:panorama" (status=failed OR result=error)
| stats count by device, job_id, admin
| sort -_time
```

## CIM SPL

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Change.All_Changes by All_Changes.action, All_Changes.object_category, All_Changes.user | sort - count
```

## Visualization

Table (failed jobs), Timeline.
